title: Sabulina muscorum
taxonomy: Sabulina muscorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a succulent plant that belongs to the genus Sabulina. Sabulina muscorum is closely related to the genus Arenaria, with some sources considering it a synonym of Arenaria species. The plant's classification is often debated among botanists, with some considering it a distinct species. Further research is needed to fully understand its classification within the Caryophyllaceae family.

intro: Sabulina muscorum, also known as Sabulina or Sandwort, is a small, succulent perennial plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress, improving skin health, and supporting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Sabulina muscorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Sabulina muscorum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and support overall well-being.
